sections.paragraph:
ἀλλάσσω (ἄλλος) fut. ἀλλάξω; 1 aor. ἤλλαξα; 2 fut. pass. ἀλλαγήσομαι (Aeschyl.+; DELG I 64 s.v. ἄλλος).

① to make someth. other or different, change, alter τὴν φωνήν μου change my tone Gal 4:20 (Artem. 2, 20 of ravens πολλάκις ἀλλάσσειν τ. φωνήν; TestJos 14:2 ἀλλ. τὸν λόγον; Just., A I, 9, 2 τὸ σχῆμα). Of the hyena τὴν φύσιν change its nature B 10:7 (s. Windisch, Hdb. ad loc.). τὰς χρόας change colors of stones Hs 9, 4, 5; 8. Of Jesus on the day of final judgment ἀλλάξει τὸν ἥλιον καὶ τὴν σελήνην καὶ τοὺς ἀστέρας he will change the sun, the moon, and the stars, so that they lose their radiance B 15:5. τὰ ἔθη change the customs Ac 6:14 (Diod S 1, 73, 3 τὰς τῶν θεῶν τιμὰς ἀλλάττειν).—Pass. (Dionys., Perieg. [GGM II, p. 127, 392]; Herm. Wr. 1, 4; 13, 5; Jos., Ant. 2, 97 v.l.; SibOr 3, 638; 5, 273 ἕως κόσμος ἀλλαγῇ of the last times; Ar. 5, 1 ὕδωρ … ἀλλασσόμενον χρώμασι): of the change in the bodily condition of Christians on the Last Day be changed 1 Cor 15:51f (s. MDahl, The Resurrection of the Body ’62, 103–5); of the change to be wrought by Christ in the heavens when the world is destroyed Hb 1:12 (Ps 101:27).

② to exchange one thing for another, exchange (Aeschyl. et al.; POxy 729, 43; BGU 1141, 41; 44; Jer 2:11; Jos., Ant. 18, 237) ἤλλαξαν (v.l. ἠλλάξαντο, as in Attic usage) τὴν δόξαν τοῦ ἀφθάρτου θεοῦ ἐν ὁμοιώματι εἰκόνος they exchanged the glory of the immortal God for … Ro 1:23 (ἀ. ἔν τινι after Ps 105:20, where it renders הֵמִיר בְּ; but s. ἐν 11). Of bad stones in a bldg. (cp. PMich I, 41, 10): Hs 9, 5, 2. Of changing clothes (Appian, Bell. Civ. 5, 122 §504 τὴν ἐσθῆτα ἤλλαξεν; Gen 35:2; 2 Km 12:20) Ox 840, 19 (ASyn. 150, 113).—B. 913. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

someth. someth. = something

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

GGM GGM = Geographi Graeci Minores, various dates—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 45
